symptom: Loss NaN after 3000 steps; gradients become infinity
cause: Exploding gradients from unbounded input range; no input normalization applied
fix: Add LayerNorm to input embedding; reduce learning rate by 10x

symptom: Validation loss increases while training loss decreases (divergence)
cause: Batch normalization running statistics mismatch; train vs eval mode inconsistency
fix: Set model.eval() during validation; fix momentum in BN (0.1 was too high)

symptom: OOM crash on batch 47 of epoch 2, never batch 1
cause: Activation checkpointing disabled in transformer block; memory scales with sequence length
fix: Wrap transformer.forward with torch.utils.checkpoint

symptom: Accuracy plateaus at 0.512 (2-class), exactly random chance
cause: Model output logits never passed through softmax; raw embeddings used in loss
fix: Apply F.log_softmax in forward pass before loss computation

symptom: Memory usage grows 2GB per epoch; no OOM but swap thrashing
cause: Loss computation graph retained; no .detach() on accumulated embeddings
fix: Call loss.backward() then optimizer.zero_grad() before detach

symptom: Train loss is 0.001 but metric shows 15% error rate
cause: Loss computed on logits; metric computed on probabilities; threshold mismatch
fix: Apply softmax before computing classification metric; use consistent threshold

symptom: Gradient updates apply inconsistently; some weights frozen
cause: Parameter group learning rates set incorrectly; one group LR = 0 by mistake
fix: Verify param_groups in optimizer; set all base_lr values to same schedule

symptom: CUDA OOM at batch size 32 but batch size 31 works
cause: Reserved cache + fragmentation; attention allocates full max_seq_len matrix
fix: torch.cuda.empty_cache() between batches; use flash-attention

symptom: Loss oscillates ±0.5 around mean; never converges smoothly
cause: Learning rate 0.01 too high; AdamW beta1=0.9 too aggressive momentum
fix: Reduce LR to 0.0001; set beta1=0.95 for slower momentum

symptom: Model predicts same class for all samples after epoch 10
cause: Class imbalance uncorrected; cross-entropy biased to majority class
fix: Add class_weight parameter to CrossEntropyLoss; weight by inverse frequency

symptom: Training diverges if batch size > 64; stable if < 64
cause: Loss scaling not adjusted for batch size; gradient magnitude increases with batch
fix: Scale learning rate proportionally: LR_new = LR_old * (batch_size_new / 64)

symptom: Metric improves but loss increases (inverse correlation)
cause: Metric uses raw predictions; loss computed on sigmoid output; scale mismatch
fix: Standardize: compute both on softmax probabilities or both on logits

symptom: Model loads from checkpoint but produces different predictions
cause: Dropout not set to eval mode; random_state not seeded at inference
fix: model.eval() before inference; set seed in torch.manual_seed()

symptom: Data loader returns different batch each iteration (no shuffle)
cause: shuffle=True not set; dataset always returns samples in order
fix: Initialize DataLoader with shuffle=True for training set

symptom: Gradients stuck at exactly 0 for encoder layers
cause: Gradients blocked by detach() in forward pass; no gradient flow
fix: Remove .detach() from encoder output; ensure requires_grad=True

symptom: Training speed drops 50% after 5000 steps
cause: No gradient accumulation cleanup; old graphs retained in memory
fix: Call optimizer.zero_grad() every step; free unused tensors

symptom: Validation metric perfect (1.0) but test metric fails (0.15)
cause: Data leakage; validation set contains copies of training samples
fix: Rebuild train/val split with no overlap; shuffle before split

symptom: Loss is inf but no gradient overflow detected
cause: Model output contains exp(large_number); softmax overflow before loss
fix: Use log_softmax + NLLLoss instead of softmax + CrossEntropyLoss

symptom: GPU utilization only 20%; training takes 2x longer than expected
cause: CPU-side data loader bottleneck; num_workers=0 (single-threaded)
fix: Set num_workers=4; pin_memory=True in DataLoader

symptom: Loss increases every epoch despite low learning rate
cause: Weight decay applied incorrectly; regularization term grows unbounded
fix: Check weight decay logic; reduce weight_decay from 1e-2 to 1e-4

symptom: Model checkpoint file corrupted after save
cause: Interrupted write; no atomic file operation (torch.save not flushed)
fix: Save to temp file, then rename; use save_atomic or context manager

symptom: Batch norm statistics shift during training; eval mode differs
cause: Momentum in BN too high (default 0.9); running_mean/var not converged
fix: Set momentum=0.01 in BatchNorm layers; allow more warmup steps

symptom: Sparse tensor gradients become dense; memory balloons
cause: All-gather operation on sparse embeddings; no sparse optimizer step
fix: Use sparse_adam or SparseAdam; avoid dense gradient conversion

symptom: Learning rate schedule not decreasing; constant throughout
cause: Scheduler.step() never called; LR stays at initial value
fix: Call scheduler.step() after each epoch or batch (check scheduler type)

symptom: Model overfits drastically; train loss 0.01, val loss 2.5
cause: No dropout or regularization applied; model too large for data
fix: Add Dropout(0.5); reduce hidden dim by 50%; increase weight_decay

symptom: Training hangs; no error but CPU at 100%
cause: Dataloader worker deadlock; infinite loop in collate_fn
fix: Debug collate_fn with timeout=0; remove infinite loops or blocking ops

symptom: Gradient clipping never triggers; gradients unbounded at 100+
cause: Clip value set to 1000 (too high); clipping disabled by mistake
fix: Set max_norm=1.0 in torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_

symptom: Model checkpoint loads but training resumes with old optimizer state
cause: Optimizer state not saved; only model weights saved in checkpoint
fix: Save dict with model, optimizer, lr_scheduler, epoch; load all

symptom: Precision issues: loss correct in float64, wrong in float32
cause: Intermediate computations lose precision; accumulation in float32
fix: Use mixed precision training; torch.cuda.amp or automatic_mixed_precision

symptom: One GPU idle; multi-GPU training not scaling
cause: DataParallel; single GPU bottleneck in forward pass
fix: Switch to DistributedDataParallel; ensure synchronization

symptom: Label smoothing causes calibration drift
cause: Smoothing applied after softmax instead of before loss
fix: Apply label smoothing to targets before computing cross-entropy

symptom: Memory leak; memory grows even with constant batch size
cause: Circular reference in custom forward; loss graph not freed
fix: Break reference cycle; use weakref or torch.no_grad() in eval blocks

symptom: Predictions identical for all samples of same class
cause: Model collapsed; all weights point to same attractor
fix: Reduce learning rate by 100x; add input noise for regularization

symptom: Tokenizer produces different token IDs on different runs
cause: Random seed not set in tokenizer; shuffle=True in tokenization
fix: Set tokenizer seed; ensure vocab built deterministically

symptom: Cosine annealing scheduler resets unexpectedly mid-training
cause: Scheduler step called twice per iteration; epoch vs step mismatch
fix: Clarify scheduler call site: after optimizer.step() or after epoch loop

symptom: Backward pass fails with autograd error; graph structure invalid
cause: In-place operation on leaf tensor during forward (x += y)
fix: Replace in-place ops: use x = x + y or clone before modify

symptom: Data augmentation applied twice; examples become unrecognizable
cause: Augmentation in dataset and in dataloader; double application
fix: Remove one augmentation layer; choose single canonical location

symptom: Model inference 10x slower than training (same batch size)
cause: Dropout not disabled; stochastic forward pass during eval
fix: Ensure model.eval() called; Dropout(p) becomes identity in eval mode

symptom: Cross-entropy loss stays at ln(num_classes) for many epochs
cause: Softmax output uniform; model not updating; dead learning rate
fix: Increase learning rate 100x; reset weights; check for zero gradients

symptom: Multi-head attention produces all-zero gradients
cause: Attention mask broadcast incorrectly; all entries masked
fix: Verify mask shape and application; test with unmasked attention

symptom: Test loss lower than training loss consistently
cause: Regularization (dropout, weight decay) only in train; test runs clean
fix: Disable regularization for fair comparison; or add to test loss

symptom: Checkpoint loading succeeds but model weights are NaN
cause: File corruption during save; dtype mismatch on load
fix: Verify save integrity; specify device and dtype during torch.load()

symptom: Distributed training hangs; all ranks waiting
cause: Barrier synchronization skipped on one rank; deadlock
fix: Ensure all ranks reach dist.barrier(); no early returns before sync

symptom: Gradient accumulation produces wrong update direction
cause: Accumulated gradients not normalized by num_accumulation_steps
fix: Divide loss by num_accumulation_steps before backward()

symptom: Model underfits; both train and val loss high and flat
cause: Model capacity too low; insufficient layers or hidden dims
fix: Double hidden dimension; add residual connections; increase depth

symptom: Learning rate warmup causes initial loss spike
cause: Learning rate jumps from 0 to max instantly; no ramp-up
fix: Implement linear warmup: LR = init_LR * (step / warmup_steps)

symptom: Batch statistics inconsistent; mean/std vary per batch
cause: Normalization computed per-batch not globally; small batch variance
fix: Use LayerNorm instead of BatchNorm for sequence models

symptom: Model produces NaN loss after loading from checkpoint
cause: Checkpoint saved in eval mode; batch norm statistics frozen
fix: Reset running statistics: for m in model.modules() if BN, m.reset_running_stats()

symptom: Sampler produces duplicate samples in dataloader
cause: Random seed not set globally; sampler order non-deterministic
fix: Set np.random.seed() and torch.manual_seed() before DataLoader init

symptom: Loss oscillates with period matching batch count
cause: Gradient accumulation not scaling loss; step-level variance high
fix: Verify accumulation divisor; check gradient noise scale

symptom: Mixed precision training produces NaN
cause: Overflow in float16 computation; scale factor too high or too low
fix: Tune loss_scale in amp; use PyTorch automatic_mixed_precision

symptom: Model output has extreme values (>1e6); softmax undefined
cause: No gradient clipping in early training; instability from initialization
fix: Set gradient clipping; initialize weights from N(0, 0.01) not U(0,1)

symptom: Validation set performance worse after adding data augmentation
cause: Augmentation applied to validation set; inconsistent with test
fix: Disable augmentation for val/test splits; only augment training
